title: Overview: Health product advertising incidents - Canada.ca
content:
## Health product advertising incidents: Overview
- How to report a complaint
- Proactive monitoring – COVID-19
- Proactive monitoring – Natural health products
- About the advertising incidents table
- Our responsibilities
- Our compliance and enforcement approach

## About the advertising incidents table
Health Canada publishes health product advertising incidents to:
- ensure the incidents comply with the legislation and associated regulations and
- protect consumers and health care professionals from false, misleading or deceptive advertising
This is part of our Regulatory Transparency and Openness Framework .
The following table summarizes advertising incidents reported to Health Canada in the last 5 years and where regulatory actions were taken. These advertising incidents include ads directed to the public and/or health care professionals.
The table lists advertising incidents of authorized health products deemed non-compliant with health product advertising provisions. The non-compliances are either resolved or ongoing . Incidents related to the advertising of unauthorized health products are not listed.
Health Canada updates this table quarterly.
Pharmaceutical (prescription), controlled    substance
Pharmaceutical (prescription)
Ecotest covid-19 & influenza a/b antigen    nasal test kit
Controlled substance
Pharmaceutical (prescription)
Pharmaceutical (prescription), controlled substance
Biosyent Pharma Inc.
Controlled substance
Pharmaceutical (prescription)
Biologics (non-prescription)
The advertising incidents that are summarized in this table cover the following types of authorized health products:
- radiopharmaceuticals
- controlled substances
- natural health products
- veterinary health products
- biological drugs (including vaccines)
- prescription products for human use or veterinary use
When addressing and resolving advertising incidents, our first priority is to protect the health and safety of people in Canada. We take appropriate actions to address incidents where we find non-compliance.
If you have questions about incidents in the summary table, including their status, email us at drug-device-marketing@hc-sc.gc.ca .

## Our responsibilities
Health Canada is the national regulatory authority for health product advertisements. We oversee and enforce the regulatory requirements related to health product advertising directed to the public and to health care professionals.
We play an active role in addressing health product advertising incidents. We also work to prevent non-compliant advertising before it occurs by:
- developing policies and guidance documents
- providing regulatory and policy clarifications
- making stakeholders aware through various activities
In Canada, industry may have their advertising material reviewed and precleared by an independent APA. While this is voluntary, we encourage all advertisers to work with APAs to ensure that promotional materials comply with the relevant legislative and regulatory requirements before it is disseminated.
- How we oversee and enforce the marketing of health products
- Health Canada and advertising preclearance agencies' roles related to health product advertising

## Our compliance and enforcement approach
All advertising complaints that are received by Health Canada are triaged and prioritised according to the risk to health they may pose to the general public. In other words, higher risk complaints are given priority.
When we identify and confirm non-compliance, we take the most appropriate level of intervention based on the risk posed to the general public. For example, we send a letter to the regulated party promptly for high-risk cases, outlining the corrective measures the party must take. Most often, the party complies once they receive this letter or after we follow up.
We may take stronger action if an advertising remain non-compliant. For example, we may:
- seize the non-compliant advertising materials
- start enforcement proceedings such as seeking an injunction or fines where a court order has been breached
- such as seeking an injunction or fines where a court order has been breached
It is prohibited to sell and advertise unauthorized health products in Canada. In these cases, we will take the necessary compliance and enforcement actions to protect the health and safety of people in Canada.
Learn more about our compliance and enforcement approach .
- Stop illegal marketing of drugs and devices
- Regulating advertising of health products in Canada
